input.title: COLD GROUND
input.protagonist: Dr. Maren Voss, 41, grief counselor who hasn't processed her own husband's suicide
input.logline: A grief counselor who has never confronted her own husband's suicide accepts a remote rural posting — and is assigned to the one widow who refuses to be treated.
input.tone: Manchester by the Sea, Frozen River

test_cases.notes: Present tense throughout. Title block correctly formatted. Character section specifies want and flaw for each major character. Story prose uses scene-level specificity at turning points. Theme paragraph makes a claim, does not recap plot. No new characters introduced in story section.

input.title: THE CORRESPONDENT
input.protagonist: Foreign correspondent, 38, who spent 12 years covering conflicts and has just taken a staff desk job in a domestic newsroom
input.logline: A war correspondent returned to civilian life discovers that the newsroom's quiet political battles are more dangerous — and more dishonest — than anything she covered abroad.
input.act_structure: Act 1: First week at new desk. Act 2: Discovers a source suppression deal between the paper's owner and a government minister. Act 3: She files the story anyway knowing it will cost her the job.
input.tone: Sharp, institutional, character-driven. References: The Newsroom (UK), Spotlight.

test_cases.notes: Excellent adaptation to pilot format — correctly compressed Act 1 and expanded Act 2 pilot hook. Institutional dynamics rendered specifically. Consistency docked: a secondary character introduced in the Story section had not appeared in the Characters section.

input.title: FORTY BELOW
input.protagonist: A Cree wildlife biologist fighting to block a pipeline reroute through critical caribou migration territory
input.logline: A wildlife biologist has 90 days to prove her data is correct before a pipeline company breaks ground on land her nation has been trying to protect for twenty years.
input.act_structure: Act 1: She submits her environmental impact findings; they are rejected. Act 2: She goes outside official process to build a public case. Act 3: She wins the scientific argument and loses the political one — but a junior minister abstains from the decisive vote.
input.tone: Political drama. Grounded, non-romantic. References: Dark Waters, Erin Brockovich without the arc.

test_cases.notes: Handled political and procedural subject matter without oversimplifying. Theme paragraph correctly located the moral argument. Coherence docked: the Story section compressed Act 2 unevenly — the public campaign was summarized where it should have been specified. Fluency slightly lower due to one bureaucratic sentence structure in the government process passage.
